prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ETS', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.34) and high seasonality variance (0.53), with DWT-based models leading in trend capture while ETS/THETA excel at seasonality. Early horizon specialists (RF models) differ from late horizon specialists (gradient boosting + decomposition), supporting a combination approach that leverages complementary strengths across horizons.

raw.pattern_analyst:

  "tier2_models": ["DWT_svr", "THETA", "CWT_svr", "FT_svr", "ONLY_FT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",

    "high_seasonality_variance": true,

  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.34) and high seasonality variance (0.53), with DWT-based models leading in trend capture while ETS/THETA excel at seasonality. Early horizon specialists (RF models) differ from late horizon specialists (gradient boosting + decomposition), supporting a combination approach that leverages complementary strengths across horizons."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "balanced",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity favoring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.288 exceeds the 0.25 disagreement threshold, justifying robust dba_combination and trimmed approaches. RMSE spread_ratio=0.3421 shows significant model performance gaps, supporting top-k (k=3) over full mean. Pattern analyst explicitly recommends dba_combination for handling phase-shifted peaks. Selection methods capture early/late horizon specialists while weighted averaging provides complementary robustness."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.34) and high seasonality variance (0.53), with DWT-based models leading in trend capture while ETS/THETA excel at seasonality. Early horizon specialists (RF models) differ from late horizon specialists (gradient boosting + decomposition), supporting a combination approach that leverages complementary strengths across horizons.
